name: Don Rickles
abstract: Donald Jay Rickles (May 8, 1926 – April 6, 2017) was an American stand-up comedian and actor, known primarily for his insult comedy. His film roles include Run Silent, Run Deep (1958), Enter Laughing (1967), Kelly's Heroes (1970), and Casino (1995).

works.t: Personal Appearance, A Comedy in Three Acts

concerts.label: Toyota Comedy Festival: Don Rickles and Rita Rudner

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I gave him the Steve Allen and Regis Philbins and Don Rickles and the Milton Berles, and all the things I had something to do with my television experience.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: all the comedians – Don Rickles, Red Foxx, Fatjack Leonard; all the singers – Peggy Lee, Pearl Bailey, Lena Horne. Everybody in show business loved us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Caputo
about_quotes.evidence: I played for Milton Berle, who had a lot of music, as does Don Rickles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Caputo interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Smith
about_quotes.evidence: it was kind of before Don Rickles, his whole act was to insult people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995
